forecast-period.text: A high pressure system centred south of the Bight extends a ridge over SA. The high will move eastwards to be centred near Tasmania on Sunday and Monday but will continue to extend a ridge over SA. The ridge will weaken mid next week as a trough moves over the west and north of SA then a cold front will move across the south late Friday and early Saturday.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

Isolated light morning showers about the southern agricultural area, mostly about windward coasts and ranges. Morning frost and fog patches about the agricultural area. Maximum temperatures below average. Southeast to northeasterly winds.
Isolated light showers about the southern agricultural area, most likely about windward coasts and ranges. South to southeasterly winds tending easterly in the far west.
Mostly dry. Morning frost and fog about the agricultural area. Maximum temperatures below average, grading to above average in the southeast. Southeast to northeasterly winds.

text.p: Morning fog and patchy frost about the agricultural area. Chance of showers developing about central parts of the pastoral districts, extending into the West Coast district in the evening. Maximum temperatures below average. Southeast to northeasterly winds.

Morning fog patches about the South East districts and in the far west. Cold at first east of the ranges with areas of frost. Showers about central and western parts of the pastoral districts and the West Coast district. Maximum temperatures below average. Southeast to northeasterly winds.

Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Wednesday: mostly less than 2 mm about the agricultural area. Totals of 2 to 10 mm about parts of the west and north and isolated falls of 10 to 15 mm are possible.
